factory — Factory class¶

New in version 0.1.

Module author: Claude Gibert <claude.gibert@synopticview.co.uk>

The Factory class¶

class core.factory.Factory(dict)¶

The Factory class registers functions and classes with an associated identifier either a string or anything hashable.

Factory(noduplicates = True, hasdefault = True)¶
Parameters:
  • noduplicates – if True, a IndexError is raised if the user attempts to register the same identifier twice. Otherwise the second register overrides the first one.
  • hasdefault – if True, when an identifier is not found by the default functions, the special string ‘__default__’ is searched. If it has been registered, then any unknown identifier will trigger the creation of what was registered under the ‘__default__’ keyword.
register(name, what)¶
Parameters:
  • name (a hashable object) – the identifier used to later instanciate an object
  • what – a class or a callable object

Registers a callable object under the name name. Any hashable object can be used for name. What is a callable object.

create(what, *args, **kargs)¶

Finds the entry under the name what and calls the object stored there with the arguments *args, **kargs. Whatever is returned by the callable object is returned by this function. If there is no default, and what is not found, an IndexError is raised.

get(what):

This is identical to create() except that it does not instanciate classes or call functions, it just returns the callable which was registered in the factory.

class core.factory.SingletonFactory(Factory)¶

Inherits the behaviour from Factory. The difference is that when create is called for an identifier, the result is cached. Whenever it is called again with the same identifier, the cache is returned. This results in creating singletons if a class is registered.

An example:

from semperpy.core.factory import Factory, SingletonFactory

class IntWrapper(object):

    def __init__(self,value):
            self.value = value

    def __str__(self):
            return str(self.value)

    def increment(self):
            self.value += 1

def no(*args,**kargs):
    raise IndexError('This is the default function')


f = Factory()
f.register('int',IntWrapper)
f.register('__default__',no)

i = f.create('int',2)
print i
2

s = SingletonFactory()
s.register('int',IntWrapper)
i = s.create('int',2)
print i
2
i.increment()
print i
3
j = s.create('int',2)
print j
3

f.create('unknown')
Traceback (most recent call last):
IndexError: Factory does not know how to create "unknown"
